name: Hacienda Community Development Corporation
description_short: Latino-led nonprofit providing HUD-approved housing counseling, financial coaching, and affordable housing development across Oregon and Washington since 1992.
description_long:
Hacienda Community Development Corporation was founded in 1992 as a mission-driven nonprofit dedicated to advancing social and economic mobility within Latino and underserved communities. Over three decades, the organization has built 911 housing units and served 4,019 residents and clients in fiscal year 2025, establishing itself as a comprehensive community development entity rather than a traditional lender.

Hacienda's core offerings span four pillars: (1) Housing services including HUD-approved housing counseling through their "Camino A Casa" program for first-time homebuyers, default intervention for homeowners at risk of foreclosure, and apartment availability in affordable communities; (2) Real estate development of mixed-use, mixed-income affordable housing across Oregon and Washington; (3) Small business services including the Portland Mercado, business coaching, access to capital, and an affordable commissary kitchen; and (4) Youth and family services including after-school programs, parenting support, digital literacy, and STEM programming.

What distinguishes Hacienda is its integrated community development approach—it is not primarily a lender but rather a nonprofit housing counselor and real estate developer that also provides financial coaching and small business support. The organization is explicitly HUD-approved for housing counseling and employs financial coaches as part of its homeownership support department. The Portland Mercado serves as both a cultural hub and practical business incubator supporting 140+ local small business owners. Their work is deeply embedded in serving primarily low- and moderate-income families within specific geographic communities they manage.

For consumers, Hacienda is best suited as a free or low-cost resource for housing education, financial coaching, and homeownership preparation rather than emergency lending. The organization's strength lies in personalized, relationship-based support for underserved populations. However, those seeking immediate capital or loans should understand Hacienda's primary function is counseling and community development, not direct consumer lending.
pros: HUD-approved housing counseling agency providing free or low-cost financial coaching to first-time homebuyers
Nonprofit structure with 33-year track record serving 4,019+ clients annually with no profit motive
Integrated support system: housing counseling paired with affordable housing access in company-owned properties
Offers default intervention and foreclosure prevention assistance for struggling homeowners at no predatory costs
Latino-led organization with culturally responsive services and multilingual support (Spanish/English)
Established small business ecosystem (Portland Mercado) providing direct capital access and business coaching to 140+ entrepreneurs
Comprehensive youth and family services embedded within housing communities, supporting residents holistically
cons: Not a direct lender—no emergency loans or quick cash products available; primarily educational and advisory
Services appear geographically limited to Oregon and Washington residents
Website provides limited detail on specific loan terms, interest rates, or approval timelines for homeownership financing through 'Hacienda Community Lending'
Small business capital access described generically; no specifics on loan amounts, terms, or APR for business borrowers
Services focus on affordable housing and community members—not designed for general population seeking commercial lending
best_for: First-time homebuyers in Oregon/Washington seeking HUD-approved counseling and financial coaching
Homeowners facing foreclosure or default who need intervention and preservation assistance
Latino entrepreneurs and small business owners seeking affordable kitchen space, business coaching, and capital access
Low- to moderate-income families seeking affordable rental housing within Hacienda-managed communities
diagnosis: Hacienda is best for low- to moderate-income homebuyers and entrepreneurs in Oregon/Washington seeking free or affordable HUD-approved housing counseling, financial coaching, and community-embedded support—not emergency lending. The primary caveat is that Hacienda is a nonprofit community development organization, not a consumer lender; those needing rapid capital or loans should seek specialized lending products elsewhere, though Hacienda may help prepare borrowers for traditional financing.
services: HUD-approved housing counseling and financial coaching for first-time homebuyers (Camino A Casa)
Default intervention and foreclosure prevention assistance for homeowners
Hacienda Community Lending for first-time homebuyer financing
Affordable apartment rental access in 10+ managed communities
One-on-one small business support services with access to capital
Financial coaching and business advising for entrepreneurs
Portland Mercado marketplace and vendor support for 19+ permanent businesses and 140+ small business owners
After-school academic support and cultural enrichment programs (Expresiones)
Parenting support groups, workshops, and home visits (Sembrando Semillitas)
Digital literacy and STEM programming for youth and adults (Arrobas: Connect Initiative)
Resident support services and community programming
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
